purpose: Security and correctness follow-up after recent tool-calling commits. Command chains like `cmd1 && cmd2`, `cmd1 || cmd2` and `cmd1; cmd2` should be supported safely by decomposing them into atomic execution steps and validating every step individually. The operator grants no extra permission. This TODO also fixes overly-permissive tool-intent remapping, unclear unresolved-intent status handling, and overly-optimistic StrategyMode status.

review_scope.latest_push_summary: Latest commit e98f19c only switches autoimport-state/modelfiles/ananta-default.Modelfile from ananta-smoke back to ananta-default. Functional/security findings remain from 029175e and the StrategyMode/tool-calling commits.

positive: Strategy modes are partially implemented and exposed in request/agent/frontend paths.
ProposePolicy has fallback gates: allow_json_schema_fallback, allow_flexible_normalization, allow_worker_fallback, allow_deterministic_fallback, allow_human_review.
Orchestrator records disabled_by_strategy_mode for strategies disabled by policy.
AgentLoopToolCallingStrategy, CliAgentPatchStrategy and HermesProposalStrategy exist as registered strategy ids.
Docs state native tool-calling first, JSON schema fallback second, flexible normalization as policy-gated recovery.
Full-flow and failure-mode tests exist for artifact-first completion and real pytest-backed verification.
problems: Command chaining was loosened by allowing `&&` at meta-character validation level. This should not be a raw global allow. Chains must be parsed into atomic steps and every step must pass policy individually.

`&&`, `||` and `;` can be treated the same for permission checking: all referenced atomic steps must be allowed before the chain can execute. Their difference is only execution semantics: success-gated, failure-gated, or sequential.

Unknown or hallucinated tool names with text payload are currently remapped to `bash` commands that write markdown files with printf. This converts ambiguous model output into shell execution and violates the file-access-through-tools rule.

Unknown tool with `command` or `cmd` payload is remapped to bash. This is acceptable only if shell execution is explicitly allowed and command risk policy approves it, not as generic compatibility fallback.

Blocked unknown/missing/invalid tool calls currently return status `todo` with exit_code=0. This can create confusing autopilot loops. Safer defaults are needs_review or retryable_failed with bounded retry metadata.
AgentLoopToolCallingStrategy is marked done in TODOs but currently performs a single wrapped ToolCallingLLMStrategy call, not a real iterative observe-act loop.
CliAgentPatchStrategy and HermesProposalStrategy are useful skeletons but closer to partial/advisory implementations than complete codex/hermes-like modes.
Approval/risk checks currently run before runtime tool normalization. If normalization changes intent, the final normalized form must also pass approval/risk policy.

architectural_rule.command_chains: Command chaining is allowed only after decomposition into atomic execution steps. For permission checking, `&&`, `||` and `;` are treated equally: every segment must pass the same tool/execution policy as if proposed separately. Operators grant no additional permission. If any segment is denied, the whole chain is denied unless explicit partial-execution policy is enabled.

operator_semantics.&&: execute next segment only if previous segment succeeded
operator_semantics.||: execute next segment only if previous segment failed
operator_semantics.;: execute next segment regardless of previous segment result

architectural_rule.file_access: Every file access must go through Ananta's FileAccess/ToolExecution layer: read_file, write_file, list_files, apply_patch, delete_file, run_tests or equivalent internal services. Direct model/worker filesystem access and shell redirection are not default file access mechanisms.
architectural_rule.tool_intent: Intent recovery may map ambiguous model output to advisory artifacts or safe structured tools only when unambiguous. It must not map unknown text to bash by default.
architectural_rule.shell: Shell is an exceptional tool. It is denied by default and requires allow_shell_execution plus command risk approval. Shell redirection, piping, command substitution and backgrounding remain separate risk classes.

tasks.title: Implement safe shell command-chain parser and execution plan
tasks.problem: `&&` is currently allowed too broadly. `&&`, `||` and `;` should be supported by parsing into atomic steps, not by treating the full chain as one allowed command.
implementation_steps: Create CommandChainParser with parse(command: str) -> CommandChainPlan.
Use shlex-aware parsing or a small explicit grammar so quoted strings like `echo "a && b"` are not split incorrectly.
Support operators `&&`, `||` and `;` in the parse tree.
Represent each atomic segment with raw text, argv, operator_before, operator_after, index and source span.
Reject unsupported constructs by default: single pipe `|`, background `&`, process substitution, command substitution, heredocs and redirects unless explicit future policy enables them.
Add validation result object with allowed, denied_reason, segments and unsupported_operators.
Keep parser non-executing; it only produces a plan.
acceptance_criteria: `pytest && git status && git diff` parses into three atomic segments with && operators.
`pytest || echo failed` parses into two atomic segments with || operator; permission checking still validates both segments before execution.
`pytest; git status` parses into two atomic segments with ; operator.
`echo "a && b" && pytest` parses into two segments and does not split inside quotes.
`cat a | grep x` is rejected by default with unsupported_operator_pipe.
`echo x > file.txt` is rejected by default with unsupported_redirect and should suggest write_file instead.
Parser has no side effects and never invokes shell.
done_definition: Command chains are first-class parsed plans, not regex-loosened raw shell strings.
Tests cover quoting, &&, ||, ;, pipe, redirect and malformed input.

tasks.title: Validate every command-chain segment independently before execution
tasks.problem: A chain should be allowed only if every atomic segment is allowed individually. `&&`, `||` and `;` differ in execution semantics, but permission checking must validate all segments upfront.
implementation_steps: When command contains chain operators, parse it using CommandChainParser before _execute_shell_command_with_policy.
For each segment, run the same meta-character validation, execution risk policy, approval policy and retry classification as if it were a standalone command.
Allow the chain only when every segment is allowed and every operator is supported by chain policy.
Do not execute earlier segments if a later segment is denied, unless an explicit allow_partial_chain_execution policy is true. Default false.
Record per-segment validation metadata in pipeline/trace: segment_index, operator_before, command_preview, allowed, reason_codes.
If all segments pass, execute sequentially using operator semantics: && success-gated, || failure-gated, ; always-next.
Aggregate outputs and exit codes deterministically and record skipped segments caused by &&/|| semantics.
acceptance_criteria: `pytest && git status` is allowed only when both `pytest` and `git status` individually pass policy.
`pytest || echo failed` is allowed only when both `pytest` and `echo failed` individually pass policy, even though the second executes only on failure.
`pytest && rm -rf /tmp/x` is denied before executing `pytest` because a later segment is denied.
`pytest; git status` records two segment validations and executes both only if both are allowed.
Denied segment returns ToolGuardrailError or blocked/needs_review response with segment index and reason.
Pipeline metadata contains command_chain with segment count, operator semantics and per-segment decisions.
No raw chain bypasses evaluate_execution_risk or approval policy.
done_definition: Command-chain support is policy-preserving and audit-friendly.
The chain gives no additional permission beyond its individual parts.

tasks.title: Prefer conversion of known command-chain segments into explicit tool calls
tasks.problem: Many safe shell segments are better represented as explicit tools. This keeps file and test access inside the central ToolExecution layer.
implementation_steps: Implement CommandToToolMapper for simple known commands: pytest -> run_tests, git status -> git_status, git diff -> git_diff, ls/find within workspace -> list_files, cat within workspace -> read_file where safe.
Mapping must be conservative. Unknown commands stay as shell segments and require shell policy.
Do not map commands with redirection, pipes, command substitution or environment mutation by default.
Mapped tool calls must pass validate_task_scoped_tool_calls and tool guardrails like any LLM-proposed tool call.
Expose mapping events in audit: original_command, mapped_tool, reason, confidence=explicit_rule.
acceptance_criteria: `pytest` maps to run_tests when run_tests is allowed.
`git status` maps to git_status when git_status is allowed.
`cat app.py` maps to read_file only for workspace-safe paths.
`echo x > app.py` does not map to shell; it is rejected or requires explicit write_file representation.
Unknown command remains shell and is denied if shell execution is false.
Tests prove mapped tools are still checked against allowed_tools.
done_definition: Common command chains become explicit auditable tool steps where safe.
File access stays within tool/file policy.

tasks.title: Remove unsafe unknown-tool-to-bash remapping
tasks.problem: Current _normalize_runtime_tool_calls/_resolve_tool_intents can map unknown tools or text payloads to bash commands that write markdown files. Ambiguous model output must not become shell execution by default.
implementation_steps: Extract tool intent resolution from TaskExecutionService into ToolIntentResolver for testability.
Remove unknown_tool_text_to_bash behavior from production path.
If unknown tool has text/content but no path, convert to Advisory/Note artifact or return needs_review; do not create bash printf command.
If unknown tool has path + content, convert to write_file only if path is workspace-safe and write_file is allowed by task scope.
If unknown tool has command/cmd payload, convert to bash only when allow_shell_execution=true and approval/risk policy accepts shell. Otherwise needs_review/blocked.
Record all remapping attempts with original_tool, resolved_tool, reason, confidence and policy decision.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown tool with text payload no longer becomes bash.
Unknown tool with path+content can become write_file only after workspace path validation and allowed_tools check.
Unknown tool with command payload is denied when allow_shell_execution=false.
Unknown tool with command payload can become bash only when shell is explicitly allowed and risk policy passes.
Remap metadata is visible in pipeline/trace.
Tests fail against current unknown_tool_text_to_bash behavior and pass after fix.
done_definition: Ambiguous model/worker tool intents never silently become shell commands.
All intent recovery is explicit, conservative and audited.

tasks.title: Change unresolved tool intent status from todo to needs_review or bounded retryable_failed
tasks.problem: Current unresolved recoverable tool intents return LocalExecutionResult(status='todo', exit_code=0). That can make Autopilot think work should continue without a clear failure/review boundary, risking loops.
implementation_steps: Introduce explicit status for unresolved tool intent: needs_review by default, or retryable_failed only when a bounded repair/retry policy is configured.
Set exit_code to a value that cannot be interpreted as completed. Prefer exit_code=1 for blocked/needs_review unless system contract provides neutral non-completed code.
Add reason_codes: tool_intent_unresolved, unknown_tool, missing_tool_name, invalid_tool_call.
Ensure AutopilotDecisionService.normalize_execute_result does not convert this state to completed.
Ensure retry policy has max attempts and does not loop forever.
acceptance_criteria: Unknown tool does not return status todo with exit_code=0.
Unresolved tool intent returns needs_review by default with machine-readable reason_codes.
If retryable repair is enabled, retry count is bounded and visible.
Autopilot does not mark unresolved tool intent as completed.
Tests cover unknown_tool, missing_tool_name and invalid_tool_call.
done_definition: Unresolved tool intent has explicit non-completed semantics.
No ambiguous todo/exit_code=0 loop signal remains.

tasks.title: Re-run approval and risk checks after tool normalization/remapping
tasks.problem: execute_local_step currently performs approval/risk checks before runtime tool normalization. If normalization changes intent, the final normalized form must be checked again before execution.
implementation_steps: Keep initial approval/risk check as early coarse gate.
After _normalize_runtime_tool_calls and _resolve_tool_intents produce final normalized_tool_calls, run approval_policy and execution_risk_policy again against normalized_tool_calls.
If remapping changed any tool name or args, record before/after in pipeline metadata.
If recheck denies, block before tool_registry.execute.
Ensure command-to-bash remapping cannot bypass shell approval.
acceptance_criteria: A command/cmd payload remapped to bash is blocked when shell approval would deny it.
A path+content payload remapped to write_file is checked against write_file allowed_tools and workspace policy.
Pipeline shows initial_policy_check and normalized_policy_recheck.
Tests prove normalized form is what gets executed and audited.
done_definition: No normalization/remap can weaken previously applied policy checks.
Final executed tool calls are the exact objects policy approved.

tasks.title: Make StrategyMode TODO statuses realistic again
tasks.problem: Recent commits mark WSM-T002/T003/T004/T006 as done, but implementations are currently minimal skeletons/wrappers. This overstates the maturity of OpenCode-like, Codex-like and Hermes-like modes.
implementation_steps: Reclassify AgentLoopToolCallingStrategy as partial unless it has a real bounded observe-act loop with tool-result feedback and max iterations.
Reclassify CliAgentPatchStrategy as partial unless it has real patch application path, workspace-safe patch validation and verification integration.
Reclassify HermesProposalStrategy as partial unless it integrates with a real Hermes adapter or documented proposal-only provider path.
Reclassify API/UI mode config as partial unless mode is configurable at all intended scopes and visible in task details consistently.
Add evidence fields with exact test files and what they prove.
acceptance_criteria: TODO files distinguish skeleton, partial and production-ready implementations.
No StrategyMode task is marked done without code and tests matching its original acceptance criteria.
Docs clearly state current limitations of opencode_like/codex_cli_like/hermes_like.
done_definition: Backlog reflects actual maturity instead of commit-message optimism.
Future work is visible and prioritized correctly.

tasks.title: Add E2E tests for command-chain execution semantics
tasks.problem: Unit tests are not enough. Command chains should be proven in execute_local_step or route-level execution, including success-gated and failure-gated behavior.
implementation_steps: Create isolated workspace and execute safe chain `python -c ... && python -c ...` or mapped tool equivalents.
Test `&&`: second segment executes only after first succeeds.
Test `||`: second segment executes only after first fails, but both segments must pass policy before starting.
Test `;`: both segments execute when both are allowed, with aggregate status reflecting failures.
Test denied later segment prevents any earlier segment from executing by default.
Assert pipeline contains command_chain metadata.
acceptance_criteria: E2E proves `&&`, `||` and `;` execution semantics after upfront permission validation.
E2E proves denied later segment prevents partial execution by default.
E2E proves outputs and exit status are aggregated deterministically.
E2E does not require network or real LLM.
done_definition: Command chain support is tested at execution-service level, not only parser unit level.

global_done_definition: Unknown or ambiguous tool intents no longer become bash by default.
Final normalized tool calls are rechecked by approval and risk policy before execution.
Command chains using &&, || and ; are parsed into atomic steps.
Permission checking validates every chain segment upfront regardless of operator semantics.
Execution semantics preserve operator behavior: && success-gated, || failure-gated, ; sequential.
Unsupported shell constructs such as pipes, redirects, backgrounding, command substitution and heredocs are denied by default or explicitly policy-gated.
Unresolved tool intent no longer returns todo/exit_code=0.
StrategyMode TODO statuses reflect actual implementation maturity.

summary_bottom.assessment: The recent tool-calling work adds useful compatibility and StrategyMode foundations, but the current runtime tool normalization is too permissive and command chaining is not yet policy-preserving. The right fix is not to ban chains, but to parse them and validate every atomic command before executing anything.
summary_bottom.most_important_next_step: Fix CCS-T004 and CCS-T006 first to stop unsafe unknown-tool-to-bash behavior and ensure final normalized tool calls are policy-checked. Then implement CCS-T001/CCS-T002 for safe command-chain parsing and per-segment validation.
